query: What are the latest updates on AI development around the Ohio?
answer: The latest updates on AI development in Ohio include a $33 billion power generation project backed by Japan, led by Bechtel and Kiewit, which will build 10 gigawatts of new energy capacity to power a data center and support AI infrastructure. This project is part of a larger U.S.-Japan Strategic Trade and Investment Agreement. Additionally, a new conservative group has launched a push for tougher AI rules, aiming to establish "common-sense" guardrails around AI development. Meanwhile, AI is also being discussed in the context of its impact on the workforce, with concerns about its effect on jobs and hiring processes.

results.title: Bechtel, Kiewit tapped for Japan-backed $33B Ohio power generation project - Construction Dive
results.content: # Bechtel, Kiewit tapped for Japan-backed $33B Ohio power generation project. The investment, first announced in October, will pour billions into Pike County, Ohio, to build 10 gigawatts of new energy capacity for a forthcoming data center. That energy will power a data center development in Pike County, Ohio. On March 21, SoftBank announced the Portsmouth Consortium — made up of major Japanese and U.S. companies — would collaborate on the large-scale power generation and AI infrastructure development. “Bechtel’s focus is integrated delivery that draws on our megaproject experience to pair gigawatt‑scale data centers with power and enabling infrastructure delivered in parallel, reducing risk and bringing AI capacity online faster, more reliably, and at the pace required to lead,” Hunt Ryan said in a statement shared with Construction Dive. The development falls under the U.S.-Japan Strategic Trade and Investment Agreement, signed in October, wherein Japan pledged a $550 billion investment into the U.S. for power, data center and manufacturing infrastructure.

An article from

# Bechtel, Kiewit tapped for Japan-backed $33B Ohio power generation project

The investment, first announced in October, will pour billions into Pike County, Ohio, to build 10 gigawatts of new energy capacity for a forthcoming data center.

Published March 26, 2026

[Zachary Phillips](/editors/zphillips/)
Editor

  + [Copy link](javascript:void(0))
  + [Email](mailto:?Subject=Bechtel%2C%20Kiewit%20tapped%20for%20Japan-backed%20%2433B%20Ohio%20power%20generation%20project&Body=https%3A//www.constructiondive.com/news/bechtel-kiewit-japan-ohio-power-generation-ai/815768/)
  + [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A//www.constructiondive.com/news/bechtel-kiewit-japan-ohio-power-generation-ai/815768/&title=Bechtel%2C%20Kiewit%20tapped%20for%20Japan-backed%20%2433B%20Ohio%20power%20generation%20project)
  + [X/Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Bechtel%2C%20Kiewit%20tapped%20for%20Japan-backed%20%2433B%20Ohio%20power%20generation%20project%20https%3A//www.constructiondive.com/news/bechtel-kiewit-japan-ohio-power-generation-ai/815768/%20via%20@constructdive)
  + [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A//www.constructiondive.com/news/bechtel-kiewit-japan-ohio-power-generation-ai/815768/)
  + [Print](javascript:window.print())
* [License](/selfservice/article-licensing/submit/?newspostUrl=https://www.constructiondive.com/news/bechtel-kiewit-japan-ohio-power-generation-ai/815768/)
* [Add us on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=https://www.constructiondive.com)

This audio is auto-generated. Please let us know if you have [feedback](/contact/).

A major [Japanese investment in artificial intelligence](https://www.energy.gov/articles/energy-department-announces-partnership-ensure-affordable-energy-and-power-americas-ai) and energy is coming to Ohio, and two major U.S. contractors are in the deal.

The U.S. Departments of Energy and Commerce last week announced a public-private partnership with Japan-based [SoftBank Group and energy provider AEP Ohio](https://www.utilitydive.com/news/doe-taps-softbank-arm-to-develop-92-gw-of-gas-generation-in-ohio/815328/). The collaboration will build 10 gigawatts of new power generation, to include 9.2 GW of natural gas generation. That energy will power a data center development in Pike County, Ohio.

On March 21, [SoftBank announced the Portsmouth Consortium](https://group.softbank/en/news/press/20260321) — made up of major Japanese and U.S. companies — would collaborate on the large-scale power generation and AI infrastructure development.

In total, 21 firms make up the consortium. Most notably for construction, that list includes Reston, Virginia-based Bechtel and Omaha, Nebraska-based Kiewit. Both contractors signed onto the trade and investment agreement in the fall with the intent on helping deliver the projects. 

Catherine Hunt Ryan, president of Bechtel Manufacturing & Technology, described the partnership as a signal that the AI market is moving from ambition to execution.

“Bechtel’s focus is integrated delivery that draws on our megaproject experience to pair gigawatt‑scale data centers with power and enabling infrastructure delivered in parallel, reducing risk and bringing AI capacity online faster, more reliably, and at the pace required to lead,” Hunt Ryan said in a statement shared with Construction Dive.

Government officials showered the project with superlatives. 

“We are going to do the [largest construction project in the country](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-20/softbank-plans-giant-ohio-ai-data-center-powered-by-gas-plants),” said Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick at the project site on March 20, Bloomberg reported. In the DOE announcement, Lutnick put the price tag of the power project at $33 billion. 

The development falls under the [U.S.-Japan Strategic Trade](https://www.constructiondive.com/news/bechtel-kiewit-us-japan-ai-infrastructure-data-centers/804220/) and Investment Agreement, signed in October, wherein Japan pledged a $550 billion investment into the U.S. for power, data center and manufacturing infrastructure.

Construction is set to begin later this year, per the DOE.

results.title: New conservative group launches push for tougher AI rules - Axios
results.content: # New conservative group launches push for tougher AI rules. A new coalition of conservative groups and advocates for stronger kids' online safety and AI laws launched Monday, as the debate over regulating AI in the U.S. heats up. * Republican infighting over the best way to regulate AI is just one hurdle the White House is going to face in trying to get their AI plans over the finish line. * The new group wants "common-sense" guardrails around AI development and is ready to put polling, money and PR campaigns into their fight. **What they're saying:** The coalition was launched to fight tech companies that are spending millions to "give AI companies regulatory and legal amnesty and ensure Washington writes the rules their way, while eliminating state protections," CEO Janet Kelly said in a release. **What we're watching:** The group's launch video focuses on public anxiety around AI. Because the richest companies in history are spending hundreds of millions to make sure there are no safeguards, no guardrails on AI," the video says.

[Skip to main content](#main-content)

[Axios](/)

50 mins ago - [Technology](https://www.axios.com/technology)

# New conservative group launches push for tougher AI rules

* [Ashley Gold](https://www.axios.com/authors/agold)

[Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com)

Add Axios as your preferred source to

see more of our stories on Google.



[Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com)

A new coalition of conservative groups and advocates for stronger kids' online safety and AI laws launched Monday, as the debate over regulating AI in the U.S. heats up.

**Why it matters:** The White House [just rolled out](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/20/white-house-ai-plan-trump-framework) a light-touch AI framework that is unlikely to satisfy strong proponents of online safety and AI rules, even as some Republicans show growing appetite for a more hands-on approach.

* Republican infighting over the best way to regulate AI is just one hurdle the White House is going to face in trying to get their AI plans over the finish line.

**Driving the news:** The coalition, Alliance for a Better Future, describes itself as "pro-innovation" and "pro-family advocacy."

* The new group wants "common-sense" guardrails around AI development and is ready to put polling, money and PR campaigns into their fight.

**What they're saying:** The coalition was launched to fight tech companies that are spending millions to "give AI companies regulatory and legal amnesty and ensure Washington writes the rules their way, while eliminating state protections," CEO Janet Kelly said in a release.

**There are ten groups in the new coalition, including:** The Family Policy Alliance, the National Center on Sexual Exploitation (NCOSE), the Institute for Family Studies, the Heritage Foundation and American Principles Project.

**Follow the money:** The group plans to spend at least eight figures in 2026 to support its advocacy efforts. That will go toward:

* Lobbying at the federal and state level.
* Releasing public advertising and education campaigns featuring "parents, creators and workers" that "highlight harmful decisions of tech executives," per the release.

**What we're watching:** The group's launch video focuses on public anxiety around AI.

* "83 percent of Americans are worried about where AI is headed. But no one's doing anything about it. Why? Because the richest companies in history are spending hundreds of millions to make sure there are no safeguards, no guardrails on AI," the video says.
* "Are we going to control AI, or is AI going to control us?"

# AI vs. Jobs: The Impact to Workforce for Students and Job Seekers

AI is making an impact on the job market with companies worth billions in automation and restructuring in their workspace. Here’s the changing of the job force for college students and job seekers in today’s market.

[Adaora Okoye](https://www.arcurrent.com/staff_name/adaora-okoye/), Staff Writer

March 26, 2026

A man shakes a humanoid robot hand, representing artificial intelligence that is going to change the systems of job hiring and opportunities for new grantees and job seekers. (Photo Courtesy of Unsplash)

Artificial intelligence is creating a big shift in the workforce, and is becoming one of the reasons people are struggling to get secure jobs. As it rapidly replaces some roles in the job market, it creates opportunities for others. These roles for skilled employees are now being replaced with new technologies. Making this change in some industries creates uncertainty within the workforce. These transformations about what’s going on in the job market on how the technology is evolving on working itself. They are also changing the restructuring of the workforce using AI tools, changing the requirements needed for seeking a job. 

According to the [World Economic Forum](https://www.weforum.org/stories/2025/04/ai-jobs-international-workers-day/), artificial intelligence with automation is creating millions of new jobs in the field of data analysis, cybersecurity and AI development. The expansion of artificial intelligence moves across industries current years, from healthcare, finance and manufacturing. Big companies are investing billions of dollars in reducing function costs making artificial intelligence a daily management of getting hired processes and workforce requirements that continues to evolve. Some workers are uncertain about job expulsion, while other individuals may see AI to be a benefit in their job department. Students and job applicants are still trying to understand the role in the workforce is changing from skilled employees roles to technology roles now. 

Article Intelligence is making it difficult in the job market leading to difficulties just to have an  interview anywhere. Many careers are already witnessing the change and struggles of the use of AI in some areas of the job market. According to the article Is A closing [the door on entry-level job opportunities? |](https://www.weforum.org/stories/2025/04/ai-jobs-international-workers-day/) they read about the percentages of new Gen Z that are job hunting.They believe that AI is discarding this education when job finding.

Ryan Grent, a computer engineering major at American River College says he has concerns about AI’s rapid effect on jobs. The large impact of the technology industry is raising uncertainty of having long-term stability due to the companies making their focus on automatic and AI being restructured in the competition of open availability.

“So many companies like Microsoft are laying off in hefty amounts with big industries like Facebook, Meta, Microsoft, and Nvidia following in their footsteps.” Grent said. 

According to [Tech Layoffs](https://news.crunchbase.com/startups/tech-layoffs/), which tracks the large-scale technology companies that lay off their employees bi-weekly, to counteract AI’s growth, some move to careers with the least risk of AI in the field but, as AI continues to evolve more, students are left with uncertainty over opportunities. Having to compete with AI other than using it as a helping guide, and only seeing it as a replacement to people affects navigating the future job market

Homaira Harris, biology major, said her main priority is going into the medical field. While her thoughts of AI being used in the medical field are limited, she does have concerns about how it’s used. With the workforce growing and changing, students must adapt to work alongside artificial intelligence to understand the technology tools in future careers. 

“My concern for Al in the field is that it’s so easy for people. If I use [AI] a lot then I’m not learning what I need to learn when I need to use those skills.” Harris said. 

According to research from the [Harvard Medical School](https://learn.hms.harvard.edu/insights/all-insights/how-artificial-intelligence-disrupting-medicine-and-what-it-means-physicians), artificial intelligence is being used in medical field service with diagnosis and treatments, analyzing X-ray anomaly and aiding treatment for the patients. 

Barry Williams, dual major for electrical engineering and certificate of engineering, says his main concern about AI is the use for hiring candidates for jobs. Many companies are now relying on artificial intelligence tools for reviewing resumes, helping to find effective candidates. Artificial intelligence has been getting concerns as the result of the person’s resumes getting outlooked especially for the qualified candidates during the hiring process. 

“AI has an enormous backdrop of people losing their jobs. I do believe AI is making it hard to get hired. A lot of these jobs are using AI to get candidates.” Williams said. 

More companies are starting to increase the use of artificial intelligence with simple and daily tasks that were once used to be human roles. Scanning resumes for applications, now used to reduce the labor costs to save time to quickly go through the resumes.  Many have concerns about how much they go through by how the company sets them up what to find on the resumes.  A lot of companies are now relying on the tool to do such a simple task that used to be a human task. The value of using AI for the role of looking for more candidates for the company is concerning due to its lack of human judgment and long-term knowledge.  With the lack of the judgment the tool has, it raises job seekers’ concerns for their resumes to be reviewed by the tool’s chance of bypassing them without trying, especially when some of the candidates are worthy to have a simple interview. 

Many employers use artificial intelligence for scanning resumes and using filter tools to identify qualified candidates. The tools they are using to get large amounts to be overlooked.

According to the [Bureau of Labor Statistics](https://www.bls.gov/), years of the rise of competition in the job market shows the data and charts of hiring and job openings.

Some graduates say they have less of a concern with AI being used in their field of the affects and the changes of the hiring process. The reactions of people that are depending on the use of AI in other other industries that rely heavily with only hands-on and in person interactions, although some still have questions about if artificial intelligence would be a threat or a useful tool for human judgment. 

Barbie Schmidt, animal science major, says AI being used in her field of veterinary is limited with the usage of artificial intelligence. Artificial intelligence use with animals has a low chance of being good use in that career field of hers. 

“I think that it’s less personal and people just rely on Al, they can get through things quicker. That’s dismissive of people who would like to be hired.” Schmidt said. 

According to the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, many use the help of AI filter hiring on resumers. 

The upcoming of artificial intelligence making changes to the job market, making challenges including the changes of the hiring process. Many of the new graduates and others are uncertain about the future of careers with AI in the process of being used for certain careers.  

As artificial intelligence is dominating companies and job skills, they still need to have human skills, especially judgment. If these tools continue, the rise of uncertainties of getting hired would change the futures of careers. With the already known outcome of using artificial intelligence as the role that was once a human task is now covering it, the results of using the tool are making them overlook and filtering out some of what they need in a candidate’s resume..  

“I do believe AI is making it hard to get hired,” Williams said.

# Tornado Watch Alerts Issued in 4 States

---

Published

Mar 26, 2026 at 05:54 PM EDT



By Anna Skinner

Senior Reporter

[Newsweek is a Trust Project member](https://www.newsweek.com/editorial-guidelines)

[National Weather Service](/topic/national-weather-service) (NWS) meteorologists have [issued a tornado watch](https://www.newsweek.com/tornado-warning-forecast-threat-map-indiana-illinois-ohio-11734267) in four states ahead of severe weather expected to trek across the Midwest and Ohio Valley beginning on Thursday evening.

The new urgent alerts come only hours after the NWS Storm Prediction Center issued an updated severe weather outlook on Thursday morning, [warning of an "enhanced risk" of severe storms](https://www.newsweek.com/map-reveals-tornado-storm-path-shift-as-risk-level-issued-indiana-illinois-ohio-11741640) in Illinois, Indiana, and Ohio. As of 5:30 p.m. local time, [tornado](/topic/tornado) watches have been issued in Ohio, far western New York, and parts of Pennsylvania and northern West Virginia.

The incoming [storms](/topic/storms) could be fueled by above-average temperatures that were in place across the region on Thursday, NWS meteorologist Matt Barnes told *Newsweek*.

## Ohio Counties Under Tornado Watch

Allen, Ashland, Ashtabula, Auglaize, Belmont, Carroll, Columbiana, Coshocton, Crawford, Cuyahoga, Defiance, Delaware, Erie, Fulton, Geauga, Guernsey, Hancock, Hardin, Harrison, Henry, Holmes, Huron, Jefferson, Knox, Lake, Licking, Logan, Lorain, Lucas, Mahoning, Marion, Medina, Mercer, Morrow, Muskingum, Ottawa, Paulding, Portage, Putnam, Richland, Sandusky, Seneca, Shelby, Stark, Summit, Trumbull, Tuscarawas, Union, Van Wert, Wayne, Williams, Wood, and Wyandot are under a tornado watch until 11 p.m.

...

## New York Counties Under Tornado Watch

Only one tornado watch went into New York. It was issued for Ripley, New York, and would remain in place until 11 p.m. local time.

## West Virginia Counties Under Tornado Watch

Brooke, Hancock, and Ohio counties are under a tornado watch until 11 p.m. local time.

## Pennsylvania Counties Under Tornado Watch

Allegheny, Armstrong, Beaver, Butler, Clarion, Crawford, Erie, Lawrence, Mercer, Venango, Washington, and Westmoreland counties are under a tornado watch until 11 p.m. local time.

## Tornado Watch vs. Tornado Warning

A tornado watch means conditions are favorable for tornadoes to develop, and people should stay alert and be ready to act if the weather worsens. A tornado warning, however, means a tornado has been spotted or detected by radar and poses an immediate threat, so residents in the warned area should take shelter right away. In short, a watch urges preparation, while a warning requires immediate action to protect life.

In addition to the tornado watches, severe thunderstorm watches were issued in Iowa, Illinois, and Indiana. A severe thunderstorm warning was issued in Ohio and Pennsylvania.
